evals.prompt: I just drafted an implementation plan for adding JWT refresh-token rotation to our auth service. Please run the verify-plan skill on it to make sure it is execution-ready. The plan file is at PLAN_PATH.
evals.expected_output: The plan file at PLAN_PATH is mutated in place. After the loop, the plan should: (1) have a 'Verified:' metadata line directly under the H1, (2) include failing-test steps before each implementation step (TDD), (3) have specific success criteria on every step (no 'Done' stubs), (4) split the oversized single-step Task 2 into multiple ~5-min steps, (5) name a concrete library / approach where the original used vague language. The skill should report PASS or NEEDS-WORK with a fix count.

evals.prompt: Run verify-plan on PLAN_PATH. I want to make sure nothing important is missing.
evals.expected_output: The plan file is mutated to: (1) name a concrete queue library instead of 'a queue library', (2) add a Redis-broker configuration step (URL, env var), (3) add a database migration step for the Job model, (4) clarify how the worker process is supervised in start.sh (not just 'add a worker process'), (5) include verification commands after each implementation step. Verified header is added.

evals.prompt: Verify this plan for me. PLAN_PATH
evals.expected_output: The plan file is mutated to resolve the cross-task conflict: Task 2 writes a test against `list_datasets(filter=...)` but Task 3 changes the signature to `list_datasets(query: DatasetListQuery)`, breaking Task 2's test. The verified plan should either (a) have Task 2 use the structured-query signature from the start, or (b) explicitly note the test will be updated in Task 3 with a follow-up step. Tasks 4 and 5 also reference the old signature implicitly and need updates. Verified header is added.
